mnemonic_summary: A single horizontal stroke = ONE.
mnemonic_visual: A single horizontal stroke — ONE finger held up flat.
mnemonic_reading: いち / いつ — counting "ichi, ni, san" starts with ichi. Kun ひと appears in 一つ (hitotsu, one thing).
etymology_story: A single horizontal stroke representing the abstract concept of "one" — the most basic counting symbol in the Chinese-Japanese script.

mnemonic_summary: Two horizontal strokes = TWO.
mnemonic_visual: Two horizontal strokes — TWO chopsticks resting parallel.
mnemonic_reading: に — "ni" is the second beat in counting. Kun ふた lives in 二つ (futatsu, two things).
etymology_story: Two horizontal strokes — a direct doubling of 一. The top stroke is shorter by tradition.

mnemonic_summary: Three horizontal strokes = THREE.
mnemonic_visual: Three horizontal strokes — THREE fingers raised.
mnemonic_reading: さん — borrowed everywhere from "Mr/Ms" (-さん) to 三月 (sangatsu, March). Kun み in 三つ (mittsu).
etymology_story: Three horizontal strokes — extending the 一 / 二 series. Used since oracle-bone script (~1200 BCE).

mnemonic_summary: Four legs (儿) inside a box = FOUR.
mnemonic_visual: A box with two legs sticking down — FOUR legs of a table inside a frame.
mnemonic_reading: し / よん — "shi" sounds like death so よん is preferred when counting people. Both readings are common.
etymology_story: Originally a pictograph of nostrils (breath). Loaned to mean "four" by sound association in ancient Chinese.

mnemonic_summary: Roman numeral V tilted between two horizontal lines = FIVE.
mnemonic_visual: A capital "I" with extra strokes — FIVE fingers locked in a tight grip.
mnemonic_reading: ご — quick and clean, as in 五月 (gogatsu, May). Kun いつ in 五つ (itsutsu).
etymology_story: Two crossed sticks between sky (天) and earth (地) — representing the five elements / five directions in early Chinese cosmology.

mnemonic_summary: A lid (亠) over a split (八) = SIX.
mnemonic_visual: A roof over a pair of legs — SIX is the legs of a SIX-pack of beer under a tavern roof.
mnemonic_reading: ろく — like rolling a SIX on a die: "rock and roll" → roku.
etymology_story: Originally a pictograph of a hut or shelter. Borrowed for "six" through phonetic loan in oracle-bone period.

mnemonic_summary: A horizontal stroke crossed by a hook = SEVEN.
mnemonic_visual: A cross with a diagonal slash — SEVEN looks like a sliced NIL.
mnemonic_reading: しち / なな — "shichi" can be misheard, so なな is common spoken-language fallback.
etymology_story: A cross-stroke indicating "to cut" — borrowed to represent the number seven. The cutting sense survives in 切 (cut).

mnemonic_summary: Two strokes splitting outward like a fan = EIGHT.
mnemonic_visual: Two strokes splaying outward — EIGHT legs of an OCTOPUS spread wide.
mnemonic_reading: はち — sharp like a bee sting (hachi = bee in Japanese!), and 8 hours is はちじかん.
etymology_story: Two strokes splaying outward like an object being split — the original meaning was "to divide." The number-eight meaning came from phonetic loan.

mnemonic_summary: A diagonal hook curling like the digit 9 = NINE.
mnemonic_visual: A hooked stroke meeting a vertical — NINE looks like a fishhook catching a fish.
mnemonic_reading: きゅう / く — "kyuu" used in counting, "ku" used in dates (九月 kugatsu, September).
etymology_story: Pictograph of a bent arm/elbow. Borrowed for "nine" by sound association. The hook stroke retains the elbow shape.

mnemonic_summary: A plus sign - Roman numeral X rotated = TEN.
mnemonic_visual: A perfect cross — TEN is two roads meeting; tally bars at TEN strokes.
mnemonic_reading: じゅう / じっ — "juu" stretches to mean "complete." Used in 十時 (juuji, 10 o'clock).
etymology_story: A simple vertical stroke (originally) meaning "completed counting on one hand" — later the horizontal crossbar was added to distinguish from 一.

mnemonic_summary: One (一) over white (白) = a HUNDRED whitewashed walls.
mnemonic_visual: One (一) stacked on white (白) — the FIRST WHITE coin: HUNDRED yen.
mnemonic_reading: ひゃく — 100 of anything. Watch for sound shift: 三百 (sanbyaku), 六百 (roppyaku), 八百 (happyaku).
etymology_story: 一 (one) on top of 白 (white / counter). Originally meant "white" but extended to mean "many" then "hundred."

mnemonic_summary: A slash (丿) over ten (十) = a THOUSAND tally marks.
mnemonic_visual: A slash crossing a ten — like THOUSAND sticks falling at a slight angle.
mnemonic_reading: せん — bills come in 千円 notes (sen-en). Kun ち in names like 千葉 (Chiba).
etymology_story: 人 (person) phonetic component with an added horizontal stroke. Used for "thousand" through phonetic loan in ancient Chinese.

mnemonic_summary: A swooping line tied off = TEN THOUSAND.
mnemonic_visual: A flag with a pole and a swooping line — TEN THOUSAND flags waving in a parade.
mnemonic_reading: まん — used in 一万 (ichiman, 10,000). Watch the price tags for 万 to denote ten-thousand.
etymology_story: Simplified from 萬, which was originally a pictograph of a scorpion (萬 = scorpion → "many" by metaphor → "ten thousand"). The modern 万 keeps only the structural skeleton.

mnemonic_summary: Inside the box (冂) is earth (土) = round YEN coin.
mnemonic_visual: A box with rounded inside — a coin in a wallet pocket. ROUND like the YEN coin.
mnemonic_reading: えん — every yen price ends in 円. Also means "round" — an えんりょ-shaped coin.
etymology_story: Simplified form of 圓 (round / circle). The original 圓 = 囗 (enclosure) + 員 (member) — a circular grouping. Used for the Japanese yen since 1871 because coins are round.

mnemonic_summary: A box with a stroke inside = the SUN, a DAY.
mnemonic_visual: A box with a line through the middle — the SUN at noon, light splitting the sky.
mnemonic_reading: にち / じつ / ひ / び — Sunday is にちようび. The kun ひ appears in 日 (hi, day) and 毎日 (mainichi, every day).
etymology_story: Pictograph of the sun with a dot/line in the middle representing the sunspot (or the sun's "essence"). The original oracle-bone form was a circle with a dot.

mnemonic_summary: A crescent shape = the MOON, a MONTH.
mnemonic_visual: A crescent shape with two horizontal lines — the MOON inside a window frame.
mnemonic_reading: げつ / つき — Monday is げつようび. Kun つき: 月 (tsuki, moon).
etymology_story: Pictograph of a crescent moon. The two horizontal lines inside represent moon phases / lunar markings. Used for both "moon" and "month" since calendars were lunar.

mnemonic_summary: Flames flickering upward = FIRE.
mnemonic_visual: Sparks flying outward from a central point — FIRE leaping with two side flames.
mnemonic_reading: か / ひ — Tuesday is かようび. Kun ひ: 火事 (kaji, fire emergency) but also ひ: 火 (hi, fire).
etymology_story: Pictograph of flames rising upward. The two outer strokes are the flickering flame tongues; the inner shape is the main fire body.

mnemonic_summary: A central drop with splashes = WATER.
mnemonic_visual: A central drop with splashes either side — WATER spreading on impact.
mnemonic_reading: すい / みず — Wednesday is すいようび. Kun みず is the base word for "water."
etymology_story: Pictograph of flowing water — a central stream with drops/spray on either side. The vertical line is the water current.

mnemonic_summary: A trunk with branches and roots = TREE.
mnemonic_visual: A vertical trunk with branches outward and roots downward — a TREE.
mnemonic_reading: もく / き — Thursday is もくようび. Kun き: 木 (ki, tree); also 木曜日 (mokuyoubi).
etymology_story: Pictograph of a tree: the vertical line is the trunk, the upper crossbar represents branches, and the lower flaring strokes are the roots.

mnemonic_summary: A person (人) over a king (王) with two specks of gold = METAL/GOLD.
mnemonic_visual: A roof with two diamonds inside — GOLD nuggets hidden under the roof of a cave.
mnemonic_reading: きん / かね — Friday is きんようび. Kun かね: お金 (okane, money).
etymology_story: A roof (人) over earth (土) holding two precious dots — representing metal ore buried in the ground. Came to mean "gold" specifically then "money."

mnemonic_summary: A cross stuck in the ground = EARTH.
mnemonic_visual: A cross with a bottom line — a stake driven into the EARTH with the line marking ground level.
mnemonic_reading: ど / つち — Saturday is どようび. Kun つち: 土 (tsuchi, soil).
etymology_story: Pictograph of a small mound of earth on a baseline — the cross with a horizontal base. The horizontal line is the ground; the upright is the soil heap.

mnemonic_summary: Sun (日) + feathers (羽) + bird (隹) - DAY OF THE WEEK named after a celestial body.
mnemonic_visual: Sun (日) + wings + bird (隹) — the day BIRD soars across the SKY each WEEKDAY.
mnemonic_reading: よう — only meaningful in 曜日 (youbi, day-of-week). Memorize the chunk: 月曜日 / 火曜日 ...
etymology_story: 日 (sun) + 翟 (pheasant feathers) — the "shining sun" element. Originally meant "to shine"; came to denote weekdays because each day was named after a celestial body.

mnemonic_summary: A stalk grown over time = a YEAR.
mnemonic_visual: A person leaning on a stick — an old man carrying the weight of YEARS.
mnemonic_reading: ねん / とし — 一年 (ichinen, one year). Kun とし: 今年 (kotoshi, this year).
etymology_story: Originally a person (人) carrying grain (禾) — a year was one harvest cycle. The modern simplification compresses the grain-bundle into the lower strokes.

mnemonic_summary: Sun (日) + temple (寺, time-keeper) = TIME / hour.
mnemonic_visual: Sun (日) + temple (寺) — the temple bell rings out the TIME of day.
mnemonic_reading: じ / とき — clock times: 三時 (sanji, 3 o'clock). Kun とき: そのとき (sono toki, at that time).
etymology_story: 日 (sun) + 寺 (temple). The temple kept time by the sun. Originally meant "season / proper moment"; came to mean "time / hour" generally.

mnemonic_summary: Splitting (八) with a knife (刀) = MINUTE / divide.
mnemonic_visual: Eight (八) over sword (刀) — divide a thing by cutting eight ways. A MINUTE is a divided slice of an hour.
mnemonic_reading: ふん / ぷん / ぶん — minutes shift voicing: 一分 (ippun), 三分 (sanpun). Also ぶん in 自分 (jibun, oneself).
etymology_story: 八 (split apart) + 刀 (knife / sword) — a knife splitting something into parts. Hence the meaning "to divide / portion / minute (1/60 of an hour)."

mnemonic_summary: Mark cutting in two = HALF.
mnemonic_visual: Eight on top + cow below cut down the middle — HALF of a cow.
mnemonic_reading: はん — 半分 (hanbun, half) and 三時半 (sanji-han, 3:30). Always はん.
etymology_story: 八 (split) on top of 牛 (cow) — splitting a cow in two for shared sacrifice / banquet. Hence "half."

mnemonic_summary: A person under a roof at this very instant = NOW.
mnemonic_visual: A roof over a small mark — sheltering RIGHT NOW under the present moment.
mnemonic_reading: こん / いま — 今日 (kyou, today!) and 今週 (konshuu, this week). Kun いま: 今 (ima, now).
etymology_story: A roof (人) over a tongue/object (≡ inverted 口) — "what is currently under cover / what is being uttered." Hence "now / present moment."

mnemonic_summary: A person (人) over a mother (母) - every child has one = EVERY.
mnemonic_visual: A person bent forward + 母 mother — every mother bends to her child EVERY day.
mnemonic_reading: まい — only in compounds: 毎日 (mainichi, every day), 毎週 (maishuu, every week), 毎年 (maitoshi).
etymology_story: 人 (person) over 母 (mother) — every person born of a mother, hence "every / each one." The 母 element is the structural anchor.

mnemonic_summary: Walking (辶) around (周) the calendar = WEEK.
mnemonic_visual: Walking radical (⻌) + 周 around — a WEEK is what you walk around in seven days.
mnemonic_reading: しゅう — 今週 (konshuu, this week), 来週 (raishuu, next week). Always しゅう.
etymology_story: 辶 (move / walk) + 周 (cycle / circle). Originally meant "to walk a circuit"; came to denote a recurring 7-day cycle = a week.

mnemonic_summary: A pestle pointing down at noon = NOON.
mnemonic_visual: A horizontal line with a pestle below — at NOON the sun pounds straight down on the ground.
mnemonic_reading: ご — only in 午前 (gozen, AM) and 午後 (gogo, PM). Solid ご.
etymology_story: Pictograph of a pestle for grinding rice — originally meant "to oppose / cross." Borrowed for the seventh earthly branch (representing noon, when the sun is "crossed" at zenith).

mnemonic_summary: A person (亻) asking what is possible (可) = WHAT.
mnemonic_visual: A person (亻) + 可 can — when a person CAN'T name something, they ask "WHAT?"
mnemonic_reading: なに / なん — なに alone (何ですか) but なん before some sounds (何時, nanji). Watch the shift!

mnemonic_summary: Two legs walking = a PERSON.
mnemonic_visual: Two strokes leaning into each other — a PERSON walking on two legs.
mnemonic_reading: じん / にん / ひと — country + 人 = nationality (日本人 nihonjin). Kun ひと: あの人 (ano hito).

mnemonic_summary: Power (力) in the rice field (田) = MAN.
mnemonic_visual: Field (田) on top + power (力) below — a MAN providing POWER to the FIELD.
mnemonic_reading: だん / おとこ — 男の人 (otoko no hito, man). Also 男性 (dansei, male).

mnemonic_summary: A figure with crossed legs sitting gracefully = WOMAN.
mnemonic_visual: A figure with crossed legs gracefully kneeling — a WOMAN sitting in seiza.
mnemonic_reading: じょ / おんな — 女の人 (onna no hito, woman). 女性 (josei, female).

mnemonic_summary: A baby with arms outstretched and swaddled = CHILD.
mnemonic_visual: A baby with arms out and legs swaddled — a CHILD bundled tight.
mnemonic_reading: し / こ — kun こ in 子供 (kodomo, child). On し in 男子 (danshi).

mnemonic_summary: Two crossing strokes - paternal authority figure = FATHER.
mnemonic_visual: Two crossed arms over a base — FATHER holding everything together.
mnemonic_reading: ふ / ちち / とう — Polite お父さん (otousan), humble 父 (chichi). On ふ in 父親 (chichioya).

mnemonic_summary: A figure of a nursing mother — the two emphasized dots originally depicted breasts, signaling "mother" by the act of nursing.
mnemonic_visual: A figure with two prominent dots — MOTHER with caring eyes (or feeding her child).
mnemonic_reading: ぼ / はは / かあ — Polite お母さん (okaasan), humble 母 (haha). On ぼ in 母国 (bokoku, motherland).

mnemonic_summary: Two hands clasped = FRIEND.
mnemonic_visual: Two crossed hands — FRIENDS shaking hands or high-fiving.
mnemonic_reading: ゆう / とも — 友達 (tomodachi, friend). On ゆう in 友人 (yuujin).

mnemonic_summary: A person (儿) striding ahead = PREVIOUS / earlier.
mnemonic_visual: A leg stepping forward, leading the way — going FIRST and AHEAD.
mnemonic_reading: せん / さき — 先生 (sensei, teacher = "born ahead"). Kun さき: 先 (saki, ahead).

mnemonic_summary: A plant sprouting from the soil = LIFE / birth.
mnemonic_visual: A sprout pushing through the earth — LIFE BEING BORN.
mnemonic_reading: せい / しょう / い / う / なま — 学生 (gakusei, student), 生まれる (umareru, to be born). Hugely flexible kanji.

mnemonic_summary: Five fingers spreading from a wrist = HAND.
mnemonic_visual: A hand with three fingers and a thumb — HAND raised.
mnemonic_reading: しゅ / て — 手 (te, hand) — direct and frequent. On しゅ in 手段 (shudan, method).

mnemonic_summary: Mouth-shape (口) over stop (止) = FOOT (where you stop).
mnemonic_visual: Top mouth + bottom path — using your FEET to walk and TALK.
mnemonic_reading: そく / あし / た — 足 (ashi, foot/leg). 足りる (tariru, to be enough).

mnemonic_summary: A vertical eye with pupil-bars = EYE.
mnemonic_visual: A vertical eye, the iris in the middle — EYE rotated 90°.
mnemonic_reading: もく / め — Kun め: 目 (me, eye), very common. 目的 (mokuteki, purpose) for the on.

mnemonic_summary: A square opening = MOUTH.
mnemonic_visual: A square box opening — an open MOUTH.
mnemonic_reading: こう / くち / ぐち — 入り口 (iriguchi, entrance), 人口 (jinkou, population).

mnemonic_summary: A flexed arm muscle = POWER.
mnemonic_visual: A bicep flexed — pure muscle POWER.
mnemonic_reading: りょく / ちから — 力 (chikara, strength). 体力 (tairyoku, physical strength).

mnemonic_summary: A child (子) under a roof (冖) with knowledge crowning down = STUDY.
mnemonic_visual: A roof over a child reaching for knowledge — STUDYING under a school.
mnemonic_reading: がく / まな — 学校 (gakkou, school), 学生 (gakusei, student). Kun まな: 学ぶ (manabu).

mnemonic_summary: Tree (木) where people meet (交) = SCHOOL.
mnemonic_visual: Tree (木) + 交 cross — buildings made of wood standing in CROSSED rows: a SCHOOL campus.
mnemonic_reading: こう — 学校 (gakkou). Always こう.

mnemonic_summary: A line at the root of a tree (木) = ORIGIN / book.
mnemonic_visual: A tree (木) with a bar at the base — the ROOT of the tree, the ORIGIN, a BOOK at its core.
mnemonic_reading: ほん / もと / ぼん / ぽん — 日本 (Nihon, Japan), 本 (hon, book). Kun もと: 本 (moto, origin).

mnemonic_summary: Speech (言) of myself (吾) = LANGUAGE.
mnemonic_visual: Speech (言) + 五 five + 口 mouth — five mouths SPEAKING a LANGUAGE.
mnemonic_reading: ご — 日本語 (nihongo), 英語 (eigo). Always ご.

mnemonic_summary: A jewel (玉) inside borders (囗) = COUNTRY.
mnemonic_visual: Border (囗) around 玉 jade — a COUNTRY is a treasure surrounded by a border.
mnemonic_reading: こく / ごく / くに — 国 (kuni, country). 中国 (chuugoku, China).

mnemonic_summary: People (人) gathering in clouds (云) = MEET.
mnemonic_visual: A roof over 云 cloud — people MEETING under a roof, MEETING at the cloud level.
mnemonic_reading: かい / あ — 会社 (kaisha, company), 会議 (kaigi, meeting). Kun あ: 会う (au, to meet).

mnemonic_summary: Altar (礻) on the earth (土) where people gather = COMPANY / shrine.
mnemonic_visual: Spirit altar (示) + 土 earth — the COMPANY shrine where workers pray to the earth-god.
mnemonic_reading: しゃ — 会社 (kaisha, company), 社員 (shain, employee). Always しゃ.

mnemonic_summary: Mouth (口) over shell-money (貝) - a paid speaker = MEMBER / employee.
mnemonic_visual: Mouth (口) over 貝 shell-money — the MEMBER who counts shells with mouth open.
mnemonic_reading: いん — 会社員 (kaishain), 駅員 (ekiin, station-staff), 店員 (tenin, shop clerk).

mnemonic_summary: A person with arms stretched wide = BIG.
mnemonic_visual: A person (人) with arms outstretched — "It was THIS BIG!"
mnemonic_reading: だい / たい / おお — 大学 (daigaku, university), 大きい (ookii, big).

mnemonic_summary: A line through the middle of a box = MIDDLE.
mnemonic_visual: A box with a vertical line piercing through the MIDDLE — bull's eye.
mnemonic_reading: ちゅう / なか — 中国 (chuugoku, China), 中 (naka, inside).

mnemonic_summary: Three small specks scattered = SMALL.
mnemonic_visual: A central stroke with two flecks — SMALL grains of rice on either side.
mnemonic_reading: しょう / ちい / こ / お — 小さい (chiisai, small), 小学校 (shougakkou, primary school).

mnemonic_summary: A mark above the baseline = UP.
mnemonic_visual: A bar above the ground line — UP, ABOVE.
mnemonic_reading: じょう / うえ / あ / のぼ — 上 (ue, above), 上手 (jouzu, skilled), 上る (noboru, to climb).

mnemonic_summary: A mark below the baseline = DOWN.
mnemonic_visual: A bar below the ground line — DOWN, BELOW.
mnemonic_reading: か / げ / した / さ / お — 下 (shita, below), 地下 (chika, underground).

mnemonic_summary: A hand (ナ) holding a tool (工) - natural left-hand grip = LEFT.
mnemonic_visual: A hand sweeping left over a workbench — your LEFT hand at the workshop.
mnemonic_reading: さ / ひだり — 左 (hidari, left). 左右 (sayuu, left and right).

mnemonic_summary: A hand (ナ) over a mouth (口) - natural right-hand bite = RIGHT.
mnemonic_visual: A hand bringing food to the mouth — your RIGHT hand eats for you.
mnemonic_reading: ゆう / う / みぎ — 右 (migi, right). 右側 (migigawa, right side).

mnemonic_summary: In FRONT, knife (刂) ready, sharing meat (月) with a smile (丷一).
mnemonic_visual: Horns (or a moon) in BEFORE-sky — what comes BEFORE.
mnemonic_reading: ぜん / まえ — 前 (mae, in front). 午前 (gozen, AM), 前回 (zenkai, previous time).
etymology_story: 止 (foot) above a boat (舟) — originally "to advance / go forward." Came to mean "front / in front" and "before (in time)."

kun_yomi: うし, あと, のち
mnemonic_summary: A traveler (彳) trailing behind = AFTER.
mnemonic_visual: Walking radical + thread + bottom — the THREAD comes BEHIND on the path.
mnemonic_reading: ご / こう / あと / うし / おく — 後 (ato, after). 午後 (gogo, PM), 後ろ (ushiro, behind).

mnemonic_summary: Evening (夕) divination (卜) - done OUTSIDE the home.
mnemonic_visual: Evening (夕) + divination (卜) — fortune-telling done OUTSIDE.
mnemonic_reading: がい / げ / そと / ほか — 外国 (gaikoku, foreign country), 外 (soto, outside).

mnemonic_summary: Sun (日) rising behind a tree (木) = EAST.
mnemonic_visual: Sun (日) caught in a tree (木) — the sun rising in the EAST through the trees.
mnemonic_reading: とう / ひがし — 東京 (Toukyou, Tokyo, "Eastern capital"). Kun ひがし: 東 (higashi).

mnemonic_summary: A bird returning to its nest at sunset = WEST.
mnemonic_visual: A bird-cage at sunset — birds returning to the cage in the WEST.
mnemonic_reading: せい / さい / にし — 西 (nishi, west). 西洋 (seiyou, the West/Western).

mnemonic_summary: A bell-shape facing the warm sun direction = SOUTH.
mnemonic_visual: A roof, a samurai banner — banners flying SOUTH in summer wind.
mnemonic_reading: なん / みなみ — 南 (minami, south). 南極 (nankyoku, Antarctica).

mnemonic_summary: Two figures back-to-back facing away from the cold = NORTH.
mnemonic_visual: Two figures back-to-back — turning their backs to the cold NORTH wind.
mnemonic_reading: ほく / きた — 北 (kita, north). 東北 (Touhoku, the northeast region).

mnemonic_summary: Sun (日) shining through a gate (門) = INTERVAL / between.
mnemonic_visual: Gate (門) with sun (日) inside — the SPACE BETWEEN two walls where light enters.
mnemonic_reading: かん / けん / あいだ / ま — 時間 (jikan, time-space), 間 (aida, between).

mnemonic_summary: Three peaks of a MOUNTAIN range.
mnemonic_visual: Three peaks rising in a row — MOUNTAIN ridge.
mnemonic_reading: さん / やま — 富士山 (Fujisan, Mt. Fuji), 山 (yama, mountain).

mnemonic_summary: Three flowing lines of a RIVER.
mnemonic_visual: Three flowing vertical strokes — water FLOWING DOWN a RIVER.
mnemonic_reading: せん / かわ / がわ — 川 (kawa, river). 川辺 (kawabe, riverside).

mnemonic_summary: A grid of irrigation channels = RICE PADDY.
mnemonic_visual: A grid of four rice paddies — RICE FIELDS divided by levees.
mnemonic_reading: でん / た / だ — common in surnames: 田中 (Tanaka), 山田 (Yamada).

mnemonic_summary: Drops falling from a cloud-shaped roof = RAIN.
mnemonic_visual: A roof with four drops falling — RAIN coming down outside.
mnemonic_reading: う / あめ — 雨 (ame, rain). 大雨 (ooame, heavy rain).

mnemonic_summary: The biggest thing (大) under the line (一) = HEAVEN / sky.
mnemonic_visual: A person (大) reaching above — touching the SKY/HEAVENS.
mnemonic_reading: てん / あま / あめ — 天気 (tenki, weather), 天才 (tensai, genius).

mnemonic_summary: Steam (气) rising = SPIRIT / mood / air.
mnemonic_visual: Steam rising — the air ENERGY surrounding things.
mnemonic_reading: き / け — 元気 (genki, energetic), 天気 (tenki, weather), 病気 (byouki, sickness).

mnemonic_summary: Grass (艹) transforming (化) = FLOWER.
mnemonic_visual: Grass (艹) + 化 change — a sprout CHANGED into a FLOWER.
mnemonic_reading: か / はな — 花 (hana, flower). 花見 (hanami, flower viewing).

mnemonic_summary: A cave (穴) of work (工) - empty above = SKY / empty.
mnemonic_visual: Cave (穴) + 工 work — the EMPTY SKY carved out above.
mnemonic_reading: くう / そら / から / あ — 空 (sora, sky), 空気 (kuuki, air), 空く (aku, to become empty).

mnemonic_summary: Rain (雨) hitting a paddy (田) - lightning = ELECTRICITY.
mnemonic_visual: Rain (雨) + 田 lightning streak — ELECTRICITY: lightning in the rain.
mnemonic_reading: でん — 電車 (densha, train), 電話 (denwa, phone), 電気 (denki, electricity).

mnemonic_summary: A wheel with axle and bed = VEHICLE / car.
mnemonic_visual: A wagon viewed from above — the body and two wheels of a CAR.
mnemonic_reading: しゃ / くるま — 自動車 (jidousha, automobile), 車 (kuruma, car).

mnemonic_summary: Walking (辶) with one's head (首) up = ROAD / way.
mnemonic_visual: Walking radical + neck (首) — keeping your NECK aimed at the ROAD ahead.
mnemonic_reading: どう / みち — 道 (michi, road), 北海道 (Hokkaidou). 茶道 (sadou, tea ceremony "way").

mnemonic_summary: Under a roof (广), claim a spot (占) = SHOP.
mnemonic_visual: Shed (广) + 占 fortune — fortune-tellers SET UP SHOP under a roof.
mnemonic_reading: てん / みせ — 店 (mise, shop). 店員 (tenin, shop clerk), 喫茶店 (kissaten, café).

mnemonic_summary: Horse (馬) measured (尺) - a relay STATION.
mnemonic_visual: Horse (馬) + 尺 measure — STATIONS are where horses were measured/changed.
mnemonic_reading: えき — 駅 (eki, station). 東京駅 (Toukyou-eki). Always えき.

mnemonic_summary: A roof over good things to eat = EAT.
mnemonic_visual: A roof over rice — sheltering the FOOD beneath.
mnemonic_reading: しょく / く / た — 食べる (taberu, to eat), 食事 (shokuji, meal).

mnemonic_summary: Food (飠) and a yawn (欠) - DRINK to wash it down.
mnemonic_visual: Eat-radical (食) + 欠 lack — you lack water, so you DRINK.
mnemonic_reading: いん / の — 飲む (nomu, to drink), 飲み物 (nomimono, drink).

mnemonic_summary: Eye (目) on legs (儿) - looking around = SEE.
mnemonic_visual: An eye (目) on legs (儿) — eyes that walk around to SEE everything.
mnemonic_reading: けん / み — 見る (miru, to see/watch). 意見 (iken, opinion).

mnemonic_summary: Ear (耳) at the gate (門) = HEAR.
mnemonic_visual: Gate (門) + ear (耳) — putting your EAR to the GATE to LISTEN.
mnemonic_reading: ぶん / もん / き — 聞く (kiku, to listen/ask). 新聞 (shinbun, newspaper).

mnemonic_summary: Speech (言) selling (売) ideas = READ.
mnemonic_visual: Speech (言) + 売 sell — READING ALOUD to sell the words to the audience.
mnemonic_reading: どく / よ — 読む (yomu, to read). 読書 (dokusho, reading books).

mnemonic_summary: A brush over a writing surface in daylight = WRITE / book.
mnemonic_visual: Brush (聿) + 日 page — a BRUSH WRITING on the page.
mnemonic_reading: しょ / か — 書く (kaku, to write). 図書館 (toshokan, library).

mnemonic_summary: Speech (言) using the tongue (舌) = TALK / story.
mnemonic_visual: Speech (言) + tongue (舌) — words spoken with the tongue: TALK.
mnemonic_reading: わ / はな — 話す (hanasu, to talk). 電話 (denwa, phone), 会話 (kaiwa, conversation).

mnemonic_summary: Like a tree growing toward you = COME.
mnemonic_visual: A person (人) under a tree — COME under the shade.
mnemonic_reading: らい / く / き / こ — 来る (kuru, to come). 来週 (raishuu, next week).

on_yomi: こう, ぎょう, あん
mnemonic_summary: Two walking-man halves = GO.
mnemonic_visual: A crossroads viewed from above — choose where to GO.
mnemonic_reading: こう / ぎょう / い / おこな — 行く (iku, to go). 銀行 (ginkou, bank), 行事 (gyouji, event).

mnemonic_summary: Stacked containers - sticking OUT.
mnemonic_visual: A mountain on top of a mountain — climbing OUT of the valley.
mnemonic_reading: しゅつ / すい / で / だ — 出る (deru, to leave). 出口 (deguchi, exit), 出発 (shuppatsu).

mnemonic_summary: Two strokes meeting at a point - going INSIDE = ENTER.
mnemonic_visual: Two strokes meeting at a point — entering through a doorway, GOING IN.
mnemonic_reading: にゅう / い / はい — 入る (hairu, to enter). 入口 (iriguchi, entrance), 入学 (nyuugaku).

mnemonic_summary: A person planted on the ground = STAND.
mnemonic_visual: A person standing tall on the ground — STAND UP straight.
mnemonic_reading: りつ / りゅう / た — 立つ (tatsu, to stand). 国立 (kokuritsu, national).

mnemonic_summary: A person (亻) leaning on a tree (木) = REST.
mnemonic_visual: A person (亻) leaning against a tree (木) — REST against the trunk.
mnemonic_reading: きゅう / やす — 休む (yasumu, to rest). 休日 (kyuujitsu, day off).

mnemonic_summary: Words (言) leaving the mouth = SAY.
mnemonic_visual: A mouth with words coming out in layered lines — SPEAKING multiple ideas.
mnemonic_reading: げん / ごん / い / こと — 言う (iu, to say). 言葉 (kotoba, word), 言語 (gengo, language).

mnemonic_summary: Net (四) for shell-money (貝) = BUY.
mnemonic_visual: A net (罒) over shell-money (貝) — netting up coins to BUY something.
mnemonic_reading: ばい / か — 買う (kau, to buy). 売買 (baibai, buying-and-selling).

mnemonic_summary: A tall pavilion silhouette = TALL / expensive.
mnemonic_visual: A tall pagoda silhouette — TALL/HIGH/EXPENSIVE building.
mnemonic_reading: こう / たか — 高い (takai, tall/expensive). 高校 (koukou, high school).

mnemonic_summary: A woman (女) under a roof (宀) - peaceful and CHEAP.
mnemonic_visual: A roof over a woman — woman safe at home: PEACEFUL, also CHEAP.
mnemonic_reading: あん / やす — 安い (yasui, cheap). 安心 (anshin, peace of mind).

kun_yomi: あたら, あら, にい-
mnemonic_summary: A standing tree freshly cut by an axe = NEW.
mnemonic_visual: Stand (立) + tree (木) + axe (斤) — chopping NEW wood with a fresh axe.
mnemonic_reading: しん / あたら / あら / にい — 新しい (atarashii, new). 新聞 (shinbun, newspaper).

mnemonic_summary: Ten (十) generations of stories (口) = OLD.
mnemonic_visual: Ten (十) + mouth (口) — ten generations of mouths talking: this is OLD news.
mnemonic_reading: こ / ふる — 古い (furui, old). 中古 (chuuko, used/secondhand).

mnemonic_summary: Long flowing hair on an elder = LONG.
mnemonic_visual: A figure with long flowing hair down the back — LONG and the HEAD/CHIEF.
mnemonic_reading: ちょう / なが — 長い (nagai, long). 社長 (shachou, company president).

mnemonic_summary: A ray (丿) shining on the sun (日) = WHITE.
mnemonic_visual: A simple kanji with a tail — a candle's WHITE flame casting light.
mnemonic_reading: はく / びゃく / しろ / しら — 白い (shiroi, white). 白人 (hakujin, white person).

mnemonic_summary: In the evening (夕), call out (口) the NAME.
mnemonic_visual: Evening (夕) + mouth (口) — at evening you speak your NAME.
mnemonic_reading: めい / みょう / な — 名前 (namae, name). 有名 (yuumei, famous).

mnemonic_summary: A claw (釆) ordering rice paddies (田) by NUMBER.
mnemonic_visual: Rice (米) + field (田) — taking your TURN in the rice field.
mnemonic_reading: ばん — 番号 (bangou, number), 一番 (ichiban, #1, first). Always ばん.

mnemonic_summary: Mouth (口) shouting a NUMBER / call sign.
mnemonic_visual: Mouth (口) + bent breath — calling out a NUMBER/CALL-SIGN.
mnemonic_reading: ごう — 番号 (bangou, number), 信号 (shingou, traffic signal). Always ごう.

mnemonic_summary: My (厶) grain (禾) = PRIVATE / I.
mnemonic_visual: Rice (禾) + 厶 private — your PRIVATE rice supply: I, ME.
mnemonic_reading: し / わたし / わたくし — 私 (watashi, I). On し in 私生活 (shiseikatsu, private life).
